# Associate CASS Data Scientist
...
Posted Jun 18, 2026
...
As an Associate CASS Data Scientist, you’ll help drive the analytical backbone of Southwest’s Continuing Analysis and Surveillance System (CASS), turning complex operational data into insights that strengthen safety, compliance, and reliability. You’ll develop automated workflows, build models, and apply advanced analytics—including machine learning and AI—to detect trends, assess risk, and optimize the health of our Continuous Airworthiness Maintenance Program. Partnering closely with Tech Ops, CASS Leadership, and cross‑functional stakeholders, you’ll create dashboards and predictive tools that inform critical decisions and support the ongoing improvement of our maintenance programs. By blending strong analytical methods with real‑world operational understanding, you’ll play a key role in ensuring our fleet stays safe, efficient, and ready to serve our Customers.

Design, develop, and implement machine learning models and algorithms that enhance our developer experience platform, ensuring alignment with arenaflex's overall technology strategy.

...

...

...

...
Work closely with AI engineers, software developers, product managers, and other data scientists to deliver end-to-end AI-enabled solutions.
...
Experience with training models or utilizing foundation models and fine-tuning them to meet custom business requirements.
...
. A Master's degree is strongly preferred and will be given special consideration.
...

...
Strong understanding and extensive experience with cutting-edge machine learning algorithms, deep learning architectures, statistical modeling, and large language models.
...

This includes working with NLP and LLM-based models in real production settings.

results.title: Casey Clark
highlights:
AI/ML Engineer | Manager @ CapTech
...
I'm a machine learning engineer with 4 years of experience building and deploying ML systems in production, with a strong focus on MLOps, model lifecycle automation, and cloud-native tooling. I've delivered end-to-end solutions using platforms like Azure Databricks, Vertex AI, and BigQuery, and built reusable pipelines, CI/CD workflows, and feature stores to help data science teams move faster and more reliably. My work blends machine learning engineering, data engineering, and data science to understand what’s needed, and how to scale it. I’ve developed ML infrastructure using tools like MLflow, Airflow, Docker, Azure DevOps, and Terraform, and I’m comfortable working across cloud platforms (GCP, Azure, and some AWS). I also hold the Google Cloud Professional Machine Learning Engineer certification (valid through 2025). I enjoy making ML systems actually work in production (clean code, reproducibility, automation, and clear handoffs) and I especially like projects that blend technical depth with real-world impact. Currently, I'm focused on growing my MLOps expertise and helping teams deploy models the right way. results.title: VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services | CareerSync
highlights:
# VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
...
About the Role & TeamIDC is seeking a transformative AI leader to define and execute our enterprise-wide AI strategy—embedding cutting-edge artificial intelligence across both internal operations and customer-facing products. This is a rare opportunity to shape how a global data and intelligence company harnesses AI, from agentic workflows and large language models to predictive analytics and AI-native product experiences. Reporting directly to the CTO, the VP of AI will lead a high-performing technical team, partner across business units, and drive measurable business impact through intelligent, responsible, and scalable AI solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern AI—including generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems—with the strategic acumen to align AI investments to revenue growth and competitive differentiation. IDC sits at the intersection of proprietary data, expert human insight, and global technology reach—an ideal foundation for AI transformation. Key opportunity areas include: AI-augmented research & analyst productivity — deploying LLM-powered tools to accelerate data synthesis, report generation, and market forecasting. Intelligent data products — embedding predictive models and AI-driven insights directly into IDC's subscription and advisory offerings. Agentic content & marketing workflows — leveraging generative AI to personalize content, automate content pipelines, and optimize Foundry's MarTech capabilities. Enterprise AI infrastructure — building shared platforms, APIs, and MLOps practices that enable AI adoption at scale across all IDC business units. What You’ll Do
...
AI Strategy & Vision: Partner with the CEO, CTO, CIO, and business unit heads to define IDC's multi-year AI roadmap—spanning generative AI, agentic systems, predictive analytics, and AI-native product development. Technical Leadership: Architect and oversee IDC's AI platform and infrastructure, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with enterprise data strategy. Product Delivery: Develop and execute IDC’s approach to agentic AI technology, thereby delivering the centerpiece of IDC Quanta, our cross-channel technology intelligence layer. Team Building: Recruit, develop, and retain a world-class AI team spanning ML engineers, data scientists, AI product managers, and MLOps specialists. Stakeholder Alignment: Translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value for executives and department heads; maintain transparent goals and resource planning. Ecosystem & Partnerships: Evaluate and manage AI technology partnerships, vendor relationships, and open-source contributions to accelerate IDC's AI capabilities. What You Bring
...
PhD. or Master's degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, Statistics, or a related quantitative field (or equivalent experience).
...
7+ years of experience in applied machine learning, AI engineering, or data science—with at least 3 years in senior technical leadership roles.
...
Demonstrated track record of
...
AI-powered products and platforms that generated measurable business impact (revenue growth, cost reduction, or product differentiation).
...
Deep expertise in modern AI/ML and agentic/generative systems design.
...
Hands-on fluency with generative AI technologies: LLM fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), prompt engineering, and agentic frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen).
...
Strong engineering foundation: Python, SQL, cloud ML platforms (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI, or Azure ML), and modern MLOps practices (CI/CD, model monitoring, feature stores).
...
Familiarity with knowledge graphs, semantic search, or AI applications in content and market research workflows.
...
AI frameworks, model
...
, and AI regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF).
...
Track record of building AI developer platforms or shared ML infrastructure used by multiple product teams.

So I did a personal not-so-technical "study" on myself and some tools to see how things go in a single exchange with an LLM.
...
I've professionally done risk and technological assessments on LLM's. This is a different effort in that this is a more personal and not so statistical journey. I do things like this with the tools I use from time to time to understand them and myself better. I wouldn't say the conclusions are shocking but, I think they are worth reading. Especially if you haven't taken the time to do something similar yourself. https://lnkd.in/eMxZRri7
...

I'm looking for a Staff Solutions Engineer for Alpha Level .  

Who are we? 
We're an early stage security startup, heavily oriented towards the application of statistical and machine learning to threat detection.  Yeah, we use some AI, but our bread and butter is bespoke self-learning models that bridge the gap from generic rules to the precision methods that are required to produce low false postive, low false negative threat detection. Our market are medium to large MSSPs and enterprises. We are rapidly picking up new customers (including household names and Fortune 100s), and it's an exciting time to be part of Alpha Level.

-SIEM experience (doesn't matter which one, we work with all of them)
...

...
-"Big Data" experience. Is that still the right buzzword?  We use large scale parallel computing, along with numerical algorithms at Petabyte per day scale.
...

...
-Worked in a startup before.  Excited for the opportunity that brings, and braced for the craziness.  This doesn't mean working tons of hours!  
-Data science-oriented.  Either worked with nerds like me, or studied mathematics/statistics/machine learning

I'm really happy to see more discussions and perspectives about the Theory of Sampling. I recommend giving this paper a read if you are or are not already ingratiated in the subject but have an interest in chemometrics or related fields. The document provides a human level discourse over the subject matter in a way that I think only Kim Esbensen can provide. I hope this becomes a growing trend in analytics domains and praxis. Great way to start the week :). A new holistic framework for ALL data analysis (incl ML, AI). It takes three domains to optimise 'data quality': sampling; analysis; chemometrics! Find it here ....

highlights:
I'm hiring at SuperMe. Most early stage startups hire for a title. We're hiring for a problem. The problem: make the product better and the network stronger at the same time. In practice, that… | Casey Winters | 50 comments
...
# Casey Winters’ Post
...
Co-founder & CEO of SuperMe
...
I'm hiring at SuperMe. Most early stage startups hire for a title. We're hiring for a problem. The problem: make the product better and the network stronger at the same time. In practice, that means: -Working with the team to shepherd product initiatives from idea to execution -Identifying and bringing the best professionals in the world onto the network -Building and nurturing communities of operators who make SuperMe more valuable for everyone -Setting up the GTM infrastructure, tooling, and processes an early stage company needs This is not a "pick a lane" role. If you've done some combination of growth, GTM engineering, community building, and product management at an early stage company, you know these things bleed into each other. You'll work directly with me and Ludo Antonov in our San Francisco office. The team is small. The surface area is large. That's the point. What I'm looking for: -You've been at an early stage company where you had to figure things out, not just execute a playbook -You can write code, write copy, and write a strategy doc. Maybe not all equally well, but you're not afraid of any of them -You have taste for what makes a professional network valuable and who makes it valuable -You're a go-getter. You don't wait to be told what to do. You identify the constraint and go fix it I don't care what your last title was. I care that you've done this kind of work and you're good at it. DM me or email me casey@superme.ai if this sounds like you.
